name: Binary Search Basics
description: Search a sorted array by repeatedly comparing with the middle element and discarding half the range.
exampleJava: while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; }
commonMistakes: Using binary search on unsorted data
Updating the wrong side after comparing with mid

name: Sorted Mid Check
description: Decide which half to keep by comparing the target with the middle value.
exampleJava: if (arr[mid] == target) return mid; else if (arr[mid] < target) left = mid + 1;
commonMistakes: Forgetting the equality case
Moving both pointers incorrectly

name: Lower Bound
description: Find the first position where a value can appear without breaking sorted order.
exampleJava: if (arr[mid] >= target) { ans = mid; right = mid - 1; }
commonMistakes: Stopping at any match instead of the first valid index
Returning the wrong insertion position when target is absent

name: Upper Bound
description: Find the first position strictly greater than the target.
exampleJava: if (arr[mid] > target) { ans = mid; right = mid - 1; }
commonMistakes: Using >= instead of >
Confusing upper bound with last occurrence

name: First and Last Occurrence
description: Run boundary-style binary searches to lock onto both ends of a value range.
exampleJava: int first = firstOccurrence(arr, x); int last = lastOccurrence(arr, x);
commonMistakes: Returning the same boundary for both ends
Not handling target absence cleanly

name: Search Insert Position
description: Return the exact position where the target belongs even if it is not present.
commonMistakes: Returning -1 when the target is absent
Returning right instead of the insertion index

name: Rotated Array Search
description: Use sorted-half logic inside a rotated array to keep discarding half the space.
commonMistakes: Assuming the whole array is globally sorted
Not identifying which half is currently sorted

name: Peak Element
description: Compare the middle with a neighbor to move toward a guaranteed peak.
exampleJava: if (nums[mid] < nums[mid + 1]) left = mid + 1; else right = mid;
commonMistakes: Using a normal exact-match binary search template
Accessing neighbors without boundary care

name: Answer Space Binary Search
description: Binary search over a numeric answer and use a feasibility check to test each guess.
exampleJava: if (isPossible(mid)) { ans = mid; right = mid - 1; } else left = mid + 1;
commonMistakes: Searching the input array instead of the answer range
Using a feasibility check that is not monotonic

name: Square Root Search
description: Search over integers to find the floor square root without overflow mistakes.
commonMistakes: Overflowing int when squaring mid
Returning the first larger square instead of the floor

name: Capacity Search
description: Binary search the smallest capacity, speed, or limit that still satisfies the problem constraints.
commonMistakes: Searching the wrong numeric range
Using a non-monotonic helper

name: Partition Binary Search
description: Partition two sorted arrays and use boundary comparisons to find the correct split.
commonMistakes: Binary searching the larger array instead of the smaller one
Using wrong partition boundaries when one side is empty
